title: 60 KILLED THE SAME DAY — JERUSALEM EMBASSY RECEIPT
title_alt: 4 PRESIDENTS SAID NOT YET. THEN JERUSALEM — 60 DEAD
KEPT: EMBASSY MOVED. HERE'S WHAT ELSE HAPPENED THAT DAY
KEPT: Embassy to Jerusalem — Same Day, 60 at Gaza Border

thumbnail.subline:
SAME DAY AS JERUSALEM
AY-PACK #2 KEPT

description:
Twenty years of presidents said not yet — keep the embassy in Tel Aviv, keep the peace talks alive. Then, on one spring afternoon in twenty eighteen, the United States cut the ribbon in Jerusalem. Before you cheer or curse that moment, you need the rest of the afternoon — because the book does not let you have the ceremony without the border.

Same afternoon: 60 killed at the Gaza border while the embassy opened in Jerusalem. AY-pack priority #2 checked off. UN vote 128–9. Promise #74 graded KEPT on the SEALED scorecard — narrow receipt, not a grade on every other promise.

📋 Full 145-promise audit: https://sealed2016.com?utm_source=youtube&utm_medium=longform&utm_campaign=embassy-v1

⏱ Chapters
0:00  Twenty years of "not yet" — then Jerusalem in one afternoon
0:15  The six-month waiver trap (1995 law)
0:50  Dec 6, 2017 — capital recognized
1:20  May 14, 2018 — ribbon cut · AY-pack priority #2
1:50  Same day — 60 killed at the Gaza border
2:20  UN General Assembly 128–9
2:50  Promise #74 KEPT (what that grade does and doesn't mean)
3:20  Full scorecard at SEALED2016.com
